name: psycopg-pool

documentation:
# Psycopg 3: PostgreSQL database adapter for Python - Connection Pool

This distribution package is an optional component of [Psycopg 3](https://pypi.org/project/psycopg/): it
contains the optional connection pool package [psycopg_pool](https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs/advanced/pool.html).

This package is kept separate from the main `psycopg` package because it is
likely that it will follow a different release cycle.

You can also install this package using:

```
pip install "psycopg[pool]"
```

Please read [the project readme](https://github.com/psycopg/psycopg#readme) and [the installation documentation](https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs/basic/install.html#installing-the-connection-pool) for
more details.

Copyright (C) 2020 The Psycopg Team

modules.name: psycopg_pool.sched
documentation:
A minimal scheduler to schedule tasks to run in the future (sync version).

Inspired to the standard library `sched.scheduler`, but designed for
multi-thread usage from the ground up, not as an afterthought. Tasks can be
scheduled in front of the one currently running and `Scheduler.run()` can be
left running without any tasks scheduled.

Tasks are called "Task", not "Event", here, because we actually make use of
`[threading/asyncio].Event` and the two would be confusing.
WARNING: this file is auto-generated by 'async_to_sync.py'
from the original file 'sched_async.py'
DO NOT CHANGE! Change the original file instead.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.sched.Scheduler.enter
documentation:
Enter a new task in the queue delayed in the future.

Schedule a `!None` to stop the execution.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.sched.Scheduler.enterabs
documentation:
Enter a new task in the queue at an absolute time.

Schedule a `!None` to stop the execution.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.sched.Scheduler.run
documentation: Execute the events scheduled.

modules.name: psycopg_pool.errors
documentation: Connection pool errors.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.errors.PoolClosed
documentation: Attempt to get a connection from a closed pool.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.errors.PoolTimeout
documentation: The pool couldn't provide a connection in acceptable time.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.errors.TooManyRequests
documentation: Too many requests in the queue waiting for a connection from the pool.

modules.name: psycopg_pool.null_pool
documentation: Psycopg null connection pool module (sync version).
WARNING: this file is auto-generated by 'async_to_sync.py'
from the original file 'null_pool_async.py'
DO NOT CHANGE! Change the original file instead.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.null_pool.NullConnectionPool.wait
documentation:
Create a connection for test.

Calling this function will verify that the connectivity with the
database works as expected. However the connection will not be stored
in the pool.

Close the pool, and raise `PoolTimeout`, if not ready within *timeout*
sec.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.null_pool.NullConnectionPool.resize
documentation:
Change the size of the pool during runtime.

Only *max_size* can be changed; *min_size* must remain 0.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.null_pool.NullConnectionPool.check
documentation: No-op, as the pool doesn't have connections in its state.

modules.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async
documentation: Psycopg connection pool module (async version).

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.wait
documentation:
Wait for the pool to be full (with `min_size` connections) after creation.

Close the pool, and raise `PoolTimeout`, if not ready within *timeout*
sec.

Calling this method is not mandatory: you can try and use the pool
immediately after its creation. The first client will be served as soon
as a connection is ready. You can use this method if you prefer your
program to terminate in case the environment is not configured
properly, rather than trying to stay up the hardest it can.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.connection
documentation:
Context manager to obtain a connection from the pool.

Return the connection immediately if available, otherwise wait up to
*timeout* or `self.timeout` seconds and throw `PoolTimeout` if a
connection is not available in time.

Upon context exit, return the connection to the pool. Apply the normal
{ref}`connection context behaviour <with-connection>` (commit/rollback
the transaction in case of success/error). If the connection is no more
in working state, replace it with a new one.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.getconn
documentation:
Obtain a connection from the pool.

You should preferably use `connection()`. Use this function only if
it is not possible to use the connection as context manager.

After using this function you *must* call a corresponding `putconn()`:
failing to do so will deplete the pool. A depleted pool is a sad pool:
you don't want a depleted pool.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._get_ready_connection
documentation: Return a connection, if the client deserves one.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.putconn
documentation:
Return a connection to the loving hands of its pool.

Use this function only paired with a `getconn()`. You don't need to use
it if you use the much more comfortable `connection()` context manager.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._maybe_close_connection
documentation:
Close a returned connection if necessary.

Return `!True if the connection was closed.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.open
documentation:
Open the pool by starting connecting and and accepting clients.

If *wait* is `!False`, return immediately and let the background worker
fill the pool if `min_size` > 0. Otherwise wait up to *timeout* seconds
for the requested number of connections to be ready (see `wait()` for
details).

It is safe to call `!open()` again on a pool already open (because the
method was already called, or because the pool context was entered, or
because the pool was initialized with *open* = `!True`) but you cannot
currently re-open a closed pool.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._ensure_lock
documentation:
Make sure the pool lock is created.

In async code, also make sure that the loop is running.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.close
documentation:
Close the pool and make it unavailable to new clients.

All the waiting and future clients will fail to acquire a connection
with a `PoolClosed` exception. Currently used connections will not be
closed until returned to the pool.

Wait *timeout* seconds for threads to terminate their job, if positive.
If the timeout expires the pool is closed anyway, although it may raise
some warnings on exit.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.resize
documentation: Change the size of the pool during runtime.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.check
documentation:
Verify the state of the connections currently in the pool.

Test each connection: if it works return it to the pool, otherwise
dispose of it and create a new one.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.check_connection
documentation:
A simple check to verify that a connection is still working.

Return quietly if the connection is still working, otherwise raise
an exception.

Used internally by `check()`, but also available for client usage,
for instance as `!check` callback when a pool is created.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.reconnect_failed
documentation: Called when reconnection failed for longer than `reconnect_timeout`.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.run_task
documentation: Run a maintenance task in a worker.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.schedule_task
documentation: Run a maintenance task in a worker in the future.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool.worker
documentation:
Runner to execute pending maintenance task.

The function is designed to run as a task.

Block on the queue *q*, run a task received. Finish running if a
StopWorker is received.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._connect
documentation: Return a new connection configured for the pool.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._add_connection
documentation:
Try to connect and add the connection to the pool.

If failed, reschedule a new attempt in the future for a few times, then
give up, decrease the pool connections number and call
`self.reconnect_failed()`.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._return_connection
documentation: Return a connection to the pool after usage.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._add_to_pool
documentation:
Add a connection to the pool.

The connection can be a fresh one or one already used in the pool.

If a client is already waiting for a connection pass it on, otherwise
put it back into the pool

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.AsyncConnectionPool._reset_connection
documentation: Bring a connection to IDLE state or close it.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.WaitingClient

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.WaitingClient.wait
documentation:
Wait for a connection to be set and return it.

Raise an exception if the wait times out or if fail() is called.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.WaitingClient.set
documentation:
Signal the client waiting that a connection is ready.

Return True if the client has "accepted" the connection, False
otherwise (typically because wait() has timed out).

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.WaitingClient.fail
documentation:
Signal the client that, alas, they won't have a connection today.

Return True if the client has "accepted" the error, False otherwise
(typically because wait() has timed out).
A position in a queue for a client waiting for a connection.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.MaintenanceTask

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.MaintenanceTask.run
documentation:
Run the task.

This usually happens in a worker. Call the concrete _run()
implementation, if the pool is still alive.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.MaintenanceTask.tick
documentation:
Run the scheduled task

This function is called by the scheduler task. Use a worker to
run the task for real in order to free the scheduler immediately.
A task to run asynchronously to maintain the pool state.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.StopWorker
documentation: Signal the maintenance worker to terminate.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.ReturnConnection
documentation: Clean up and return a connection to the pool.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.ShrinkPool
documentation:
If the pool can shrink, remove one connection.

Re-schedule periodically and also reset the minimum number of connections
in the pool.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.pool_async.Schedule
documentation:
Schedule a task in the pool scheduler.

This task is a trampoline to allow to use a sync call (pool.run_task)
to execute an async one (pool.schedule_task). It is pretty much no-op
in sync code.

modules.name: psycopg_pool._task
documentation: Task for Scheduler and AsyncScheduler

modules.name: psycopg_pool.base_null_pool
documentation: Psycopg mixin class for null connection pools

modules.name: psycopg_pool
documentation: psycopg connection pool package

modules.name: psycopg_pool.version
documentation: psycopg pool version file.

modules.name: psycopg_pool.base
documentation: psycopg connection pool base class and functionalities.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.BasePool.closed
documentation: `!True` if the pool is closed.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.BasePool.get_stats
documentation: Return current stats about the pool usage.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.BasePool.pop_stats
documentation:
Return current stats about the pool usage.

After the call, all the counters are reset to zero.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.BasePool._get_measures
documentation: Return immediate measures of the pool (not counters).

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.BasePool._jitter
documentation: Add a random value to *value* between *min_pc* and *max_pc* percent.

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.BasePool._set_connection_expiry_date
documentation:
Set an expiry date on a connection.

Add some randomness to avoid mass reconnection.

classes.name: psycopg_pool.base.AttemptWithBackoff

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.AttemptWithBackoff.update_delay
documentation: Calculate how long to wait for a new connection attempt

methods.name: psycopg_pool.base.AttemptWithBackoff.time_to_give_up
documentation: Return True if we are tired of trying this attempt. Meh.
Keep the state of a repeated operation attempt with exponential backoff.

modules.name: psycopg_pool._acompat
documentation:
Utilities to ease the differences between async and sync code.

These object offer a similar interface between sync and async versions; the
script async_to_sync.py will replace the async names with the sync names
when generating the sync version.

functions.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.aspawn
documentation: Equivalent to asyncio.create_task.

functions.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.spawn
documentation: Equivalent to creating and running a daemon thread.

functions.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.agather
documentation: Equivalent to asyncio.gather or Thread.join()

functions.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.gather
documentation: Equivalent to asyncio.gather or Thread.join()

functions.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.asleep
documentation: Equivalent to asyncio.sleep(), converted to time.sleep() by async_to_sync.

classes.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.Queue
documentation: A Queue subclass with an interruptible get() method.

classes.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.AEvent
documentation:
Subclass of asyncio.Event adding a wait with timeout like threading.Event.

wait_timeout() is converted to wait() by async_to_sync.

classes.name: psycopg_pool._acompat.ACondition
documentation:
Subclass of asyncio.Condition adding a wait with timeout like threading.Condition.

wait_timeout() is converted to wait() by async_to_sync.

modules.name: psycopg_pool._compat
documentation: compatibility functions for different Python versions

documentation:
Wait for the pool to be full (with `min_size` connections) after creation.

Close the pool, and raise `PoolTimeout`, if not ready within *timeout*
sec.

Calling this method is not mandatory: you can try and use the pool

documentation:
Context manager to obtain a connection from the pool.

Return the connection immediately if available, otherwise wait up to

methods.name: psycopg_pool.pool.ConnectionPool.open
documentation:
Open the pool by starting connecting and and accepting clients.

closed until returned to the pool.

some warnings on exit.
